text.title: Product Launch Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Product Launch. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Launch planning starts ≥6 weeks before the ship date; later starts truncate channels.
rule.rationale: Launch planning starts ≥6 weeks before the ship date; later starts truncate channels.

rule.statement: Pick ONE primary segment + ONE message per launch; multi-segment dilutes signal.
rule.rationale: Pick ONE primary segment + ONE message per launch; multi-segment dilutes signal.

rule.statement: Launch has ONE named, instrumented success metric (e.g. paid signups in week 1).
rule.rationale: Launch has ONE named, instrumented success metric (e.g. paid signups in week 1).

rule.statement: Day-of has a runbook with timestamps, owners, and rollback steps for each channel.
rule.rationale: Day-of has a runbook with timestamps, owners, and rollback steps for each channel.

rule.statement: 30-day post-launch retro is scheduled and held — kill / iterate / scale verdict.
rule.rationale: 30-day post-launch retro is scheduled and held — kill / iterate / scale verdict.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-product-launch.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
